message:
refactor(lowering): extract statement lowering with proper break/continue/switch semantics

Major refactor of the IR0→IR1 lowering pipeline:

- Extract all statement-lowering match arms from lower_ir0_to_ir1 into
  a dedicated lower_statement_to_ir1 function, eliminating ~450 lines
  of code duplication between the top-level lowering and nested blocks.

- Introduce ControlFlowTargets struct carrying optional break_label and
  continue_label, propagated through loop and switch bodies via
  lower_statement_to_ir1_with_flow. break and continue now emit Jump
  instructions to the correct labels instead of Nop.

- Illegal break/continue (outside loop/switch) now produces proper
  SemanticError with IllegalBreak/IllegalContinue codes instead of
  silently emitting Nop.

- Extract lower_switch_to_ir1 as a dedicated function with correct
  discriminant binding, dispatch table with per-case labels, proper
  default fallthrough, and break support via ControlFlowTargets.

- Factor out alloc_pattern_primary_binding to DRY up binding allocation
  for destructured patterns across variable declarations, for-in, and
  for-of.

- Update tests: lower_break_emits_nop → lower_break_outside_control_flow_is_error,
  lower_continue_emits_nop → lower_continue_outside_loop_is_error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
